codeReview.summary: Implementation is solid and follows established patterns from previous features. All five admin user management methods are correctly implemented with proper error handling, comprehensive docstrings, and thorough test coverage. The code is clean, consistent with existing database methods, and handles edge cases appropriately.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The commit includes both admin user methods AND API key methods (test_database_api_keys.py), but the feature description only mentions admin user methods. This suggests the worker may have implemented m1-f4-api-key-methods in the same commit as m1-f5-admin-user-methods.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Git diff shows 3 files changed: kiro/core/database.py (232 lines added with both API key and admin user methods), tests/unit/test_database_admin_users.py (390 lines), and tests/unit/test_database_api_keys.py (216 lines). The commit message only mentions admin user methods, but the diff clearly includes API key implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed TDD approach correctly: wrote comprehensive tests (22 test cases covering all CRUD operations, edge cases like case sensitivity and duplicate usernames, and integration scenarios), then verified all tests pass. The skill procedure was followed properly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows verification command 'py -m pytest tests/unit/test_database_admin_users.py -v' with all 22 tests passing in 2.20s. Tests were created before manual verification, following the backend-worker skill's TDD procedure.

summary: Feature m1-f5-admin-user-methods successfully implements all five admin user management methods (get_admin_user, list_admin_users, create_admin_user, update_admin_password, delete_admin_user) with comprehensive test coverage. The implementation follows established patterns, includes proper error handling for duplicate usernames, supports auto-timestamp generation, and maintains consistency with other database methods. All 22 unit tests pass, covering happy paths, edge cases (case sensitivity, nonexistent users), and integration scenarios. The code quality is high with clear docstrings and examples. Minor observation: the commit also includes API key methods which should have been a separate feature, but this doesn't affect the quality of the admin user implementation.
